name: Txoritokieta and Akolako Dolmen Loop
name_original: Dolmen Akolako Lepua I – circular desde Martutene por San Markos
description: This difficult 16.4-mile mountain biking route from Martutene explores the scenic Aiako Harria Parke Naturala, featuring the Dolmen Akolako
region_intro: Mountain biking around Saint-Sébastien offers diverse terrain, from coastal paths with views of the Bay of Biscay to lush forests and rolling hills. The region is characterized by its unique position between mountains and the sea, providing varied landscapes for exploration. Riders can find routes winding through dense woodlands, along river valleys, and ascending peaks like Monte Ulia, offering panoramic vistas. This natural setting provides a rich network of mountain bike trails suitable for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Saint-Sébastien The most popular mountain bike trail is Txoritokieta and Akolako Dolmen Loop, a 16.4 miles (26.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the scenic Aiako Harria Natural Park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tranbiabidea a La Plata – circular por Ulia Pasealekua, a difficult 5.0 miles (8.1 km) path. This route offers views along the Ulia Pasealekua and towards the coast. Local mountain bikers also love the Malbazar Pass – Irubideta Forest Path loop from Intxaurrondo, a 36.2 miles (58.3 km) trail leading through dense forest paths, often completed in about 5 hours 36 minutes. Mountain biking around Saint-Sébastien is defined by coastal cliffs, lush forests, and rolling hills, with routes offering views of the Cantabrian Sea. The network provides options for easy, moderate, and difficult rides, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Saint-Sébastien are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 3000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Saint-Sébastien's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Saint-Sébastien?
A: There are &lt;em&gt;78&lt;/em&gt; mountain bike trails around Saint-Sébastien. These routes offer a diverse range of experiences, from coastal paths to forest trails.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Saint-Sébastien?
A: The trails around Saint-Sébastien cater to various skill levels. You'll find &lt;em&gt;12 easy&lt;/em&gt; routes, &lt;em&gt;26 moderate&lt;/em&gt; routes, and &lt;em&gt;40 difficult&lt;/em&gt; routes, ensuring options for beginners to experienced riders.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on mountain bike trails in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Saint-Sébastien offers incredibly diverse landscapes for mountain biking, situated uniquely between mountains and the sea. You can expect lush green areas, rolling hills, dense forests, and meadows. Many routes provide stunning views of the Bay of Biscay, dramatic coastal cliffs, and scenic sections along the Urumea River.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails with good viewpoints in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Yes, many trails offer breathtaking views. For instance, routes around &lt;strong&gt;Monte Urgull&lt;/strong&gt; provide impressive views of La Concha Bay and the Old Town. Trails on &lt;strong&gt;Monte Igeldo&lt;/strong&gt; offer panoramic views of the Cantabrian coast, while &lt;strong&gt;Monte Ulia&lt;/strong&gt; boasts stunning vistas of the Cantabrian Sea and impressive coves. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24740637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of La Concha Beach – View from Kukuarri loop from Amara-Donostia&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route specifically designed for scenic views.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Saint-Sébastien?
A: While many trails are challenging, the region does offer options suitable for families. The &lt;strong&gt;Plazaola Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; is an excellent choice, often following a river and passing through tunnels, making it ideal for a more relaxed ride, even in warmer weather. Look for the &lt;em&gt;easy&lt;/em&gt; rated trails on komoot for suitable family options.

Q: Can I bring my dog on mountain bike trails in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Many natural areas and trails around Saint-Sébastien are generally dog-friendly, especially those away from crowded urban centers. However, it's always recommended to check local regulations or specific trail rules before heading out with your dog, particularly in protected natural parks like Aiako Harria.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078571880/txoritokieta-and-akolako-dolmen-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txoritokieta and Akolako Dolmen Loop&lt;/a&gt; explores the scenic Aiako Harria Natural Park. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077753564/tranbiabidea-a-la-plata-circular-por-ulia-pasealekua" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tranbiabidea a La Plata – circular por Ulia Pasealekua&lt;/a&gt;, offering coastal views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10526038" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malbazar Pass – Irubideta Forest Path loop from Intxaurrondo&lt;/a&gt; is a longer loop through dense forests.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Saint-Sébastien besides natural beauty?
A: Beyond the stunning natural scenery, you might encounter interesting landmarks and natural features. For instance, you could come across the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5830253" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oielekuko Borda&lt;/a&gt; hut or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4768952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Pagotzarte&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails might lead you near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/189561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña de Ataloi&lt;/a&gt; natural monument or even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6156605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kontxa Cave&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Saint-Sébastien is enjoyable for mountain biking through much of the year due to its mild climate. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, ideal for riding. Summer can be warm, but coastal breezes and shaded forest trails provide relief. Winter riding is possible, though some higher elevation trails might be affected by weather.

Q: Are there any trails that follow greenways or old railway lines?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;Plazaola Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; is a notable example. This route often follows a river and passes through tunnels, providing a unique and generally flatter riding experience, which can be particularly pleasant on hot days.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Saint-Sébastien?
A: The mountain bike trails in Saint-Sébastien are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 380 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning coastal and mountain views, and the well-maintained network of paths that cater to various skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for advanced riders?
A: Absolutely. Saint-Sébastien offers numerous challenging routes, with 40 trails rated as 'difficult'. These often include significant elevation gains and technical sections. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Witches' Bridge – Plazaola Greenway loop from Errekalde&lt;/a&gt;, for example, is a difficult route covering a substantial distance and elevation.
tips: This is a good bridge with a wide area on both sides for bicycles and pedestrians. The surrounding area is very green and pleasant. Bravo! Basque Country for such a great infrastructure.

This path runs along the side of the mountain that has the abandoned Txoritokieta fortress. The path is very pretty with beautiful views, but if you have time to go up to the fortress it is very interesting.
A great trail that leads to the Dolmen Akolako Lepua I. Nice views and easy to follow.
At this point the Akolako Lepua I dolmen doesn't look like much more than rocks on the ground but there is a plaque with more information. It makes an interesting stop when you are on an adventure.

faqs.question: Is the Dolmen Akolako Lepua I – circular desde Martutene por San Markos trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as difficult and is best suited for experienced mountain bikers with good fitness and advanced riding skills. You should be prepared for challenging sections, and potentially needing to push your bike at times.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Martutene, suggesting parking options would be available in or near the Martutene area. Specific parking lots are not detailed, but look for public parking within Martutene or near the San Markos area.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not provided, trails in natural areas like Aiako Harria Parke Naturala generally welcome dogs. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the challenging terrain and potential for wildlife, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location in Gipuzkoa, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather for mountain biking, with milder temperatures and less extreme heat or cold. Summer can be good, but be mindful of warmer temperatures. Winter rides are possible but may involve wet and muddy conditions, making the difficult sections even more challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or Aiako Harria Parke Naturala?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Dolmen Akolako Lepua I trail or to enter Aiako Harria Parke Naturala for recreational activities like mountain biking.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: As a difficult mountain bike route, expect a mix of challenging surfaces. This likely includes rocky, rooted, and steep sections. The trail's designation as one of the 'Top 10 Most Beautiful MTB Trails' suggests varied and scenic landscapes throughout the ride.

faqs.question: What are the notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several interesting points. These include the Bridge over the Urumea River, a View of the Txoritokieta Hillside, and the Dolmen Akolako Lepua I itself, which is a significant historical site.

faqs.question: What is the significance of the Dolmen Akolako Lepua I?
faqs.answer: The Dolmen Akolako Lepua I is an ancient megalithic burial chamber, indicating a rich historical and archaeological presence along the trail. Its inclusion suggests a unique cultural element to your mountain biking experience, connecting you with the region's past.

faqs.question: Is this a circular mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as indicated by its name, 'circular desde Martutene por San Markos,' this is a circular route, meaning you will start and finish in the same general area, Martutene.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. Notably, it covers sections of the Donostia - Beasain, Bidegorri, Igoringo trikuharriak, and E04 Mendibai ibilbidea: Ereñotzu - Behobia trails, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs near the trailhead in Martutene or San Markos?
faqs.answer: Martutene and the surrounding areas are residential and likely have local establishments. While specific cafes or pubs directly at the trailhead aren't listed, you should find options for refreshments and food in Martutene before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this difficult trail?
faqs.answer: For a difficult mountain bike trail of this length and elevation, you should bring plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes/patch kit), a first-aid kit, appropriate protective gear (helmet, gloves, knee/elbow pads), and navigation tools. Given the potential for pushing your bike, sturdy footwear is also recommended.
